sources.finding: AudioContext.getOutputTimestamp() relates contextTime to performanceTime at the output device; projection accuracy improves when the scheduled context time is close to the sampled output position.
outputLatency is the specification-defined estimate from host playback request to physical sample production, and may vary with platform and output hardware.

finding: A frequency match and render advance can still be coincidental. Strong acoustic evidence should require the microphone detection window to overlap a declared timing window projected from the scheduled Web Audio pulse through getOutputTimestamp(), with explicit capture-delay and tolerance bounds.

verification.runtime: Node test runner against byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: fail closed before output timestamp starts

uncertainty: The declared capture-delay range is a protocol parameter and must be calibrated per device/session.
Temporal overlap does not prove causal emitter identity or listener perception.
No iPhone Safari runtime session was performed in this run.
falsification_route: Shift the scheduled pulse, replay an off-window tone, or use a stale output timestamp. The classifier must reject temporal correlation in each case.
privacy_boundary: Only derived timing values and classifications are required; raw microphone audio, device labels, and persistent hardware identifiers are excluded.
